SEO & Search Visibility Assessment · July 2026

Knot Counseling:
the SEO assessment.

A data-backed look at knotcounseling.com. In this assessment you will see exactly what is holding the website back in search, the changes you can expect us to make, and what those changes unlock.

Prepared for Melissa Shaw, MSW, LCSW
Therapist + Owner · Knot Counseling · Serving Lakewood & Greater Denver since 2010
PREPARED BY

USE → ARROW KEY OR THE BUTTONS BELOW · CLICK ANY SCREENSHOT TO ZOOM

Session 01 · What is working

What is already working in your favor.
Every good assessment starts with the strengths.

1.75
Average position for the query "knot counseling". Google knows exactly who you are when someone asks by name.
95/100
Desktop performance score. On a computer, your site is genuinely fast. Not a single failing metric.
#11.6
Your affair recovery page already averages page two of Google. It is knocking on the door.
12+
Real, well-written FAQs on your couples page. The empathy in your copy is rare. Most therapy sites sound like brochures.
16 yrs
Practice history, DORA licensure, EMDRIA membership, Psychology Today verification. You have every trust signal Google wants. They are just not wired in yet.
3
Pages already earning impressions for money keywords (couples, affair recovery, premarital) without any technical help at all.

Here is the honest read: this is not a weak website. The practice fundamentals are strong, and the site already holds every trust signal Google looks for. The work ahead is wiring those signals in and unlocking the potential that is already there. We are refining, not rebuilding.

Session 02 · The diagnosis

Therapy is a YMYL category.
Google grades you like a hospital, not a blog.

YMYL means "Your Money or Your Life": content that can affect health, safety, or wellbeing. Mental health sits at the very top of that list. Google demands proof of Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trust before it sends vulnerable people your way. Fair enough. You would want the same.

Right now, that trust is failing on three fronts, tied into six specific knots:

Trust front 1 · Speed

The site stonewalls phones

Core Web Vitals fail on mobile, where anxious people actually search at 11pm. Knot 1.

Trust front 2 · Identity

Google sees two of you

Knot Therapy or Knot Counseling? Your own code cannot decide. And Melissa, the credentialed expert, is invisible in it. Knots 2, 3, 4.

Trust front 3 · Persuasion & proof

Pages inform, but do not convert or earn votes

Money pages read like caring blogs, and the backlinks pointing at them are links Google has quietly stopped counting. Knots 5, 6.

METHOD FOR EVERY KNOT AHEAD  →  PROBLEM · EVIDENCE · WHY IT MATTERS · SOLUTION

Session 03 · The battlegrounds

Your target pages,
and the exact keywords we take them to.

The searches your future clients are typing this month, mapped to the page that should own each one. Volume: US monthly searches, Semrush. KD: how hard page one is, lower is easier. One clear opportunity in the data: online-related keywords we can put to work, since you already offer online sessions. Every knot in this deck exists to move these targets.

Knot 01 / 06 · Core Web Vitals

Google measured your real visitors' phones.
Verdict: Failed.

Problem

Google's Core Web Vitals assessment for knotcounseling.com fails on mobile. This is not a lab simulation. It is measured from real Chrome users over the last 28 days. Desktop passes beautifully, which proves the content is fine. The mobile delivery is the issue.

Evidence
Field data, real users, 28 days · pagespeed.web.dev · Jul 2, 2026CLICK TO ZOOM +
Lab test, mobile, same dayCLICK TO ZOOM +
3.4s
Largest Contentful Paint, real users. Google's pass line is 2.5s.
1.7s
Time to First Byte. The server thinks for 1.7s before sending anything. Target: under 0.8s.
6.3s
LCP on a mid-range phone in the lab test. Six seconds of a blank hero.
95
Desktop score. Proof this is fixable delivery, not a broken site.
Same page, desktop: 95 performance, everything greenCLICK TO ZOOM +
Why it matters
  • Page experience is a ranking input on mobile, and mobile is where your clients are: someone Googling "couples counseling near me" at night is on their phone, often in a hallway or a parked car.
  • Every extra second of load measurably increases abandonment. A person in distress does not wait six seconds. They tap back and call whoever loads first.
  • Diagnosis of the cause is already done: the team section on the homepage still serves heavy PNG images while the rest of the site uses WebP, the header logo ships as a 900px PNG, and the 1.7s TTFB points at missing server caching. All three are routine fixes.
Solution
  • Images: convert the remaining PNGs (team section, 900px header logo) to WebP, sized for phones. Google's own estimate: 1,182 KiB off this page.
  • Server: full-page caching plus a CDN pulls TTFB from 1.7s to under 0.6s. Preload the hero image so the first thing a visitor needs is the first thing sent.
  • Render path: 20+ stylesheets and scripts (booking calendar, menus, two copies of jQuery) load before the page may paint. Load them only where used. Estimated savings: almost 5 seconds.
  • Polish: fix font loading so text shows instantly (470 ms flagged), strip unused CSS, delay the code Elementor loads by default.
  • Proof: re-test after 28 days of field data. Target: the badge flips to Passed.
Knot 02 / 06 · Entity identity

Google keeps asking who you are.
Your website answers: "It's complicated."

Problem

Your domain, your logo, and your headlines say Knot Counseling. But the structured data inside your own pages, the code Google reads to understand who you are, declares the business name as "Knot Therapy". Your Instagram and Facebook are @knottherapyonline. Your booking system lives on knottherapy.kartra.com. The site footer copyright says Knot Therapy too.

Evidence
// What your pages tell Google today // (schema found live on knotcounseling.com) { "@type": ["LocalBusiness", "Organization"], "name": "Knot Therapy", ← wrong name "url": "https://knotcounseling.com", "priceRange": "$$", ... } og:site_name = "Knot Therapy" socials → @knottherapyonline booking → knottherapy.kartra.com

Search engines and AI models build a knowledge graph of entities. When signals disagree, confidence splits between two half-entities: "Knot Counseling" (domain, reviews) and "Knot Therapy" (schema, socials, booking). Neither gets your full 16 years of trust.

In a YMYL category, entity confidence is not optional. It is the whole game.

Why it matters
  • Diluted entity trust means diluted rankings on every page at once. This is a sitewide handbrake, not a single-page bug.
  • AI assistants (ChatGPT, Gemini, Google AI Mode) cite entities, not pages. A split identity is why they hesitate to recommend you by name.
Solution
  • Pick the canonical name (the domain says it should be Knot Counseling) and correct it across schema, og tags, and footer in one pass.
  • Add sameAs links from the schema to the socials, Psychology Today, Nextdoor, her Influential Women feature, and DORA, telling Google explicitly "these profiles are all me."
  • Align citations over time: Google Business Profile, directories, and the booking page branding.
Knot 03 / 06 · The invisible expert

You are a licensed clinician with 16 years of experience.
To Google's code readers, you are a name with a photo.

Problem

In a YMYL niche, Google's guidelines explicitly ask: who is responsible for this advice, and what are their qualifications? The only Person markup on the site is a thin author snippet on blog posts. No license, no credentials, no specialties, and its social link is literally broken text: "Facebook.com/knotcounseling" is not a valid URL.

Evidence
// Melissa's Person schema today (blog only) { "@type": "Person", "name": "Melissa Shaw, MSW, LCSW", "sameAs": [ "https://knotcounseling.com", "Facebook.com/knotcounseling" ← invalid ] } jobTitle: missing credentials / license: missing specialties (knowsAbout): missing link to the business entity: missing
// What we deploy instead (already drafted) { "@type": "Person", "name": "Melissa Shaw", "jobTitle": "Licensed Clinical Social Worker", "hasCredential": { "name": "LCSW", "recognizedBy": "Colorado DORA", "identifier": "1774" ← verifiable license }, "knowsAbout": ["Gottman Method", "EFT", "EMDR", "Affair Recovery", ...], "worksFor": { Knot Counseling entity } }
Why it matters
  • Structured data is how Google verifies E-E-A-T at scale. A DORA-verifiable license number in machine-readable form is the strongest trust signal a solo practice can send.
  • Entity-connected schema dramatically improves how accurately AI systems describe a business. Unmarked expertise is unquotable expertise.
Solution
  • We will deploy the full Person markup above on the About page and connect it to the business identity, so Google reads Practice, Founder, Credentials, and Services as one verified chain instead of four loose facts.
  • We will extend it to every therapist on the team page, each with license type and specialties.
Knot 04 / 06 · Naked service pages

Your money pages carry no entity markup.
And your FAQ buttons confuse AI agents.

Problem

The revenue pages (Couples, Affair Recovery, Premarital, EMDR & Trauma) have no Service schema: no structured data telling Google what the service is, who provides it, and where. Some pages have FAQPage schema, but it is not connected to the Knot Counseling organization entity, so the answers carry no credentials. And the FAQ accordions are coded as headings styled to look like buttons, which screen readers and AI crawlers cannot reliably operate.

Evidence
PageSpeed "Agentic Browsing" audit: accessibility tree not well-formed on FAQ togglesCLICK TO ZOOM +
  • Couples page: 12 strong FAQs, zero connection to the code that identifies Knot Counseling. To Google, anonymous answers.
  • No MedicalBusiness or Service typing on any specialty page. Google must guess what "Couples" is.
  • FAQ toggles built as <h3 role="button"> with no accessible name: screen readers and AI agents cannot reliably open your best content.
Why it matters
  • Service schema is what lets Google map "couples counseling lakewood" to your specific page with confidence, instead of treating it as one long article.
  • AI agents that cannot parse the accordions cannot quote the answers. Those 12 FAQs are exactly what AI Overviews love to cite. Right now they are locked in a jar.
  • In 2026, pages are read by machines shopping on behalf of humans. Machine readability is part of trust.
Solution
  • Add Service schema to every revenue page: serviceType, provider linked to the Knot Counseling organization entity, and areaServed (Lakewood + online across Colorado). In plain terms, the code will say: this is couples counseling, by licensed therapists, here.
  • Connect the existing FAQPage schema to that same entity with @id references, so every answer carries your credentials with it.
  • Rebuild the accordion toggles as real <button> elements with accessible names (proper ARIA). One template edit fixes every page at once.
  • Add a 40-60 word direct answer under each page's H1: the exact snippet format search engines and AI answers quote most readily.
Knot 05 / 06 · Conversion architecture

The couples page wins readers,
but loses bookings.

Problem

The couples page is built like an article, not a landing page. The writing is genuinely excellent ("You just can't seem to get through" is a beautiful opening), but every conversion element, the booking button, the pricing, the trust signals, sits at the bottom of a 16-minute read. In SEO terms: the page can earn traffic, but its layout loses the booking. Left column below is what the page does today. Right column is what the same page becomes.

Evidence
On the page today
  • First "Schedule a Free Consultation" button: at the bottom of a 16 minute read (your own meta tags say so).
  • No pricing anchor. Cost anxiety is the number one silent objection in therapy.
  • No therapist faces or review proof near the decision point. Trust badges live in the footer, after the decision.
  • No sticky mobile call button, on a page whose visitors are mostly holding a phone.
What the same page becomes
  • Above the fold: promise + proof + action. "Couples counseling in Lakewood. Free 15-minute consultation. Sliding scale from $75." One screen, decision enabled.
  • Trust strip up top: DORA licensed · EMDRIA · Psychology Today verified · since 2010.
  • Therapist faces and a short outcome story before the first scroll ends. People buy the person, not the modality.
  • Sticky "Book a free consult" button on mobile. Depth stays below for the researchers.
The proof already exists, in your own words

Everything a decision block needs is already written on your homepage, verified live July 2, 2026. It just never made it to the pages where people decide:

  • "We don't use answering services, admins or automations." No other practice in the area leads with this.
  • "Expert insight, not just talk therapy" and "real growth, not just temporary relief." Your own headings, buried mid-homepage.
  • In-person and online, 7 days a week, day and evening. One line, two biggest objections answered, and it unlocks the online keyword opportunities from Session 03.
  • Weekend Marriage Intensives plus EMDR, ART, Gottman, EFT and ketamine-assisted therapy. A range solo competitors cannot match.
  • Supervised clinical interns as a lower-cost path. The direct answer to cost anxiety. One wording check with you, then it sits beside every booking button.

Relocating your own published claims is not a rewrite. It is moving furniture you already own into the room where guests actually sit.

Why it matters

Traffic only pays when the page converts. If rankings improve but visitors still leave without booking, half the gain is wasted. Google also measures engagement: when visitors stay and book instead of returning to the search results, those behavioral signals support rankings. Fix the layout first, and every ranking gain that follows delivers full value.

Solution
  • Nothing gets rewritten without your sign-off, and the default plan needs almost no rewriting. Your copy stays word for word. We add a decision block above it: promise, trust strip (DORA licensed · EMDRIA · Psychology Today verified · since 2010), price context, one clear button, plus a sticky mobile call button.
  • The 16 minutes of depth stays below for the researchers. Deciders served in one screen, researchers in sixteen. Same page, your voice untouched.
  • Approval is a five-minute review of one new section, not a leap of faith on your whole page.
Knot 06 / 06 · Authority & backlinks

Backlink findings:
where the current link profile stands, and how we strengthen it.

Problem

The link profile was built the old way: guest posts on generic lifestyle blogs with no connection to mental health. Many of those pages have since been dropped from Google's index entirely. A link on a page Google refuses to index passes exactly nothing.

Evidence

Every link verifiable, click any source to see it live. Index lost = Google indexed the page, then removed it. Crawled, never indexed = Google looked and refused. Either way, the link passes nothing today.

Guest post carrying your linkPoints atAnchor keywordStatus
notsalmon.com · The Road to Recovery: Overcoming Anxietyknotcounseling.com/anxiety-treatment/anxiety therapy LakewoodIndex lost
optimisticmommy.com · 4 Best Ways to Improve Your Relationshipsknotcounseling.com (homepage)marriage counseling lakewood coIndex lost
imcgrupo.com · Everything About Anxiety And How To Get Over It?knotcounseling.com/anxiety-treatment/anxiety therapy lakewoodIndex lost
medchrome.com · What Should You Know about Couples Counseling?http://knotcounseling.com/ (links to http, not https)Couples counseling lakewood coIndex lost
incrediblethings.com · 6 Ways of Building Trust in a Relationshipknotcounseling.com (homepage)marriage counseling LakewoodIndex lost

usalifesstyle.org · Reignite the Spark: Couples Therapy in Lakewoodknotcounseling.com/couples-counseling-marriage-counseling/ (legacy URL)Couples therapy LakewoodIndex lost
healthcarter.com · The Power of Talk Therapy: Anxiety in Lakewoodknotcounseling.com/anxiety-treatment/anxiety therapy LakewoodIndex lost
scubby.com · Finding Love Again: Couples Therapy in Lakewoodknotcounseling.com/couples-counseling-marriage-counseling/Lakewood couples therapyIndex lost
medsnews.com · Guiding Relationships: Couple Counseling in Lakewoodknotcounseling.com/couples-counseling-marriage-counseling/Lakewood couples counsellingIndex lost
citizensjournals.com · Healing Journey of Lakewood's Couples Therapyknotcounseling.com/couples-counseling-marriage-counseling/Lakewood couples therapyIndex lost
healthcarebusinessclub.com · Services Provided by a Counselorknotcounseling.com (homepage)marriage counseling lakewoodCrawled, never indexed
royalpitch.com · Divorce Or Counseling: How Couple Therapy Can Helpknotcounseling.com/couples-counseling-marriage-counseling/Lakewood couples counselingIndex lost
tvboxbee.org · How Will Therapy With Counselors Helpknotcounseling.com/couples-counseling-marriage-counseling/lakewood couples therapyIndex lost
medicalnewstodayblog.com · Reasons to Opt for Couples Counselingknotcounseling.com/couples-counseling-marriage-counseling/lakewood couples therapyIndex lost
contentrally.com · Neurofeedback Therapy Is Shaping the Futuregeneric mentionoff-niche domainIndex lost
dgmnews.com · Rediscovering Connectiongeneric mentionoff-niche domainIndex lost
allblogthings.com · Revitalizing Love: The Power of Marriage Intensivesgeneric mentionoff-niche domainIndex lost
clayvenues.com · How to Plan Your Weddingknotcounseling.com/blog/premarital-counseling-and-a-strong-marriage/premarital counselingIndex lost
Why those links died, and how the replacements will not

The pattern in the graveyard above is consistent: fresh articles written only to carry a link, on sites with no real readers. Google removed the pages, every link died with its page. The fix is the exact reverse:

  • Prefer link insertions into aged, indexed articles. A page Google has trusted for one to two years passes standing trust. A brand-new guest post gambles on surviving the next index cleanup.
  • Outreach only to niche-relevant sites with well-written content, so the pages stay indexed and the links keep counting. Domain relevance matters as much as page relevance: one of your live backlinks sits on tvboxbee.org, a generic TV-box domain, while your niche is YMYL mental health. A link like that carries little weight even while it survives.
  • Every placement is aimed: anchor and destination mapped to a keyword lane. Example: an aged relationship article links affair recovery counselors to knotcounseling.com/specialties/affair-recovery/, the page sitting at 11.6 with zero votes.
49/230
Of ~230 followed links reviewed, only about 49 are legitimate brand mentions worth having.
0
Links pointing at the affair recovery or trauma pages. The pages closest to ranking get zero outside votes.
35
Authority Score. Respectable, but built on volume, not relevance. Relevance is what YMYL rankings are made of.
Why it matters
  • In YMYL, a single link from a psychology publication, a Colorado news outlet, or a university outweighs fifty lifestyle guest posts.
  • Referring-domain quality is also one of the strongest predictors of whether AI assistants cite a business. The old links are invisible to both audiences.
  • The affair recovery page sits at position 11.6 with zero link support. It is the cheapest ranking win on the entire site.
Solution
  • Shift from volume to relevance: placements inside pages Google already indexes and trusts, vetted with the five checks above.
  • Aim first at the two pages within striking distance: affair recovery (11.6, zero links) and premarital (KD 3 to 17 terms open). Couples gets the second wave.
  • No link networks, no bulk marketplaces, nothing we cannot verify in Google's index ourselves. In this niche, shortcuts are how sites disappear. The table above is the receipt.
Loose threads · Quick, visible fixes

Six loose threads we found while pulling the big knots.
Threads 1 to 4 · Small fixes. Fast reviews. Trust the client can see.

1 · Hidden headings behind a "+" widget
Homepage lead magnet section · collapsed "+" toggleCLICK TO ZOOM +

Extra H3 headings sit behind a collapsed "+" toggle: headings written for Google that a visitor never sees. In 2026 this reads as hidden text, and it quietly works against every trust signal we are building. Two of them are even misspelled ("counceling"). We remove it.

2 · A typo doing damage on the trauma page
knotcounseling.com/specialties/emdr-trauma/ · live Jul 2026CLICK TO ZOOM +

The trauma page says "It be can scary" in the exact paragraph doing the emotional heavy lifting. A person deciding whether to trust you with their trauma reads that sentence. One-minute fix: "It can be scary."

3 · The author box has warmth, not proof
Current author box on blog postsCLICK TO ZOOM +

The bio is human and well written, which is right. But it carries zero verifiable signals: no LinkedIn, no license verification, no credential trail, no link to the full About bio. In YMYL the author box is a trust asset, not a formality. We wire in LinkedIn, the DORA license lookup, certifications and the About page, mirrored in her Person markup from Knot 3.

4 · A premarital post that points nowhere

knotcounseling.com/blog/premarital-counseling-and-a-strong-marriage/ repeats "premarital counseling" over and over, yet contains not a single link to the premarital counseling page it should be lifting. Right now it competes with the money page instead of feeding it.

Two honest options, both end with the internal link going in this week:

  • Deepen it into the definitive supporting article, linking down to knotcounseling.com/specialties/premarital-counseling/ on the head term.
  • Re-aim it at a long-tail lane it can own outright, such as counseling before marriage (210/mo, KD 14 easy win) or pre marriage counseling (1,900/mo, KD 20 easy win), and let the money page keep the head term clean.
Loose threads · Part 2

Two more threads.
A cluster to build, and a door to reopen.

5 · A pillar cluster hiding in plain sight

Three live posts already orbit the same painful question and do not know it: Why Couples Therapy Fails, Couples Therapy or Break Up, and Why the Same Fight Keeps Happening.

  • Create one pillar page: "When couples therapy is not working." Link the three posts to it, and link the pillar to the couples page.
  • These posts answer the questions people search before booking (does it work, what to expect). Together they can earn AI Overview citations while passing authority to the couples page.
  • New content cost: one pillar. Everything else is already written.
6 · AI crawlers are blocked at the door
knotcounseling.com/robots.txt · Cloudflare managed rules · Jul 2026CLICK TO ZOOM +

Robots.txt is not blocking normal Google crawling, so this is not the reason for current rankings. But Cloudflare-managed rules block GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, CCBot, Applebot-Extended and others. Likely intentional content protection. Still worth a review: AI-assisted discovery is now part of how people research therapy, and it feeds the empty scoreboard in Session 05.

  • The goal is not opening the site to every bot blindly. It is separating discovery access from training access.
  • Keep Google crawling open. Keep private and admin areas blocked.
  • Review the Cloudflare AI settings together: allow the trusted discovery crawlers, keep the training crawlers blocked where you want them blocked.
Session 04 · What the knots are costing

Ranking on page two: visible to Google, not yet to searchers

208
Affair Recovery impressions last month at average position 11.6. Result: 3 clicks. People searched, Google considered you, almost nobody saw you.
167
Premarital Counseling impressions at position 28.9. Result: 0 clicks. Zero. From a page that answers the exact question.
27.1K
Monthly US searches for "couples counseling", with 480 + 390 for the Denver variants and 140 for Lakewood. The demand is not the problem.

What these positions cost, in plain numbers

Your local commercial keyword set (Lakewood and Denver couples, affair recovery and premarital terms) totals roughly 1,840 searches every month. At today's average positions, almost none of those searches become visits: most clicks go to the practices sitting in the top five results. Every position these pages climb moves a share of those searchers from a competitor's booking page to yours. That is the entire business case for the six knots in one sentence.

Session 05 · The 2026 layer

Your next clients are asking ChatGPT first.
Right now, it has mentioned you once.

Semrush AI Search + SEO overview · knotcounseling.com · Jul 2026CLICK TO ZOOM +
14
AI visibility score across ChatGPT, Gemini, Google AI Overviews and AI Mode.
1
ChatGPT mention. One. For a 16-year practice with your review base.
0
Gemini mentions. Google's own assistant does not know what to say about you.

The part nobody else has noticed

Buried in your Search Console data is something remarkable. Your affair recovery page is already surfacing for raw, conversational, mid-crisis queries, the kind people type into AI chats and Google's AI Mode:

Real query, last 30 daysYour position
"how do i get over this?"3
"i had doubts"2
"questions to ask your boyfriend after he cheated on you"2
"how long is this going to take"2
"detachment in a relationship"1

Search engines already believe your content belongs in emotional, conversational moments. The entity work in Knots 2 to 4 converts that belief into being the practice AI assistants recommend by name in Lakewood and Denver. And part of the score above is self-inflicted: your own robots.txt currently blocks the AI crawlers (loose thread 6). Your competitors have not started. Being early here is worth more than being good later.

Session 06 · What we will NOT charge you for

Search Console shows ~2,000 "problem" URLs.
Here is the honest triage: most are noise.

Any agency can screenshot a wall of red from Search Console and invoice fear. Most of those 2,000 flags are Google working exactly as intended. You should know which is which, so nobody ever sells them to you.

Search Console flagWhat it actually isVerdict
Blocked by robots.txtPages you intentionally told Google not to crawl. It is obeying.Ignore
Not found (404)Old URLs that no longer exist. Normal website metabolism.Ignore
Duplicate URLs with tracking codes (?kuid=...)Marketing-tag copies of real pages. Canonical tags already tell Google which is real.Ignore, verify canonicals once
Crawled, currently not indexed:
Seven Strategies for Feeling Better From the Bottom Up
How to Be Resilient
Marriage Intensive Transforms Relationships
Are You Sabotaging Your Marriage?
EMDR Therapy
Top 10 Relationship Dos and Don'ts
 Good posts no page on the site links to (the last one is also thin). Orphan content reads as "even the site owner does not link to this." Google shelved them.Fix: internal links (some are thin content too)
Crawled, not indexed:
knotcounseling.com/specialties/emdr-therapy/		A money page Google has looked at but not filed. Part of the reason sits one row down on this page: two pages are fighting for the same job.Fix: internal links + the overlap below
The one real overlap · EMDR page vs Trauma page

Both pages are valuable. They just overlap too much. The trauma page is meant to target trauma therapy, yet it explains EMDR in detail, sessions and phases included, while a separate EMDR therapy page exists for that exact topic. Google cannot decide which page should rank for EMDR searches, so it hesitates on both.

We keep both pages, and give each a clear job: the EMDR page owns EMDR-specific searches, the trauma page owns trauma therapy and PTSD searches.

Should the trauma URL be renamed? Ideally, yes. The current address carries "emdr" in it, which keeps feeding the confusion:

  • Current: knotcounseling.com/specialties/emdr-trauma/
  • Better: knotcounseling.com/specialties/trauma-therapy/ or knotcounseling.com/specialties/trauma-therapy-lakewood/
  • With a 301 redirect from the old address, so nothing already earned is lost.

Out of two thousand flags, about six items deserve human attention. That ratio is the difference between an audit and a scare tactic.

Session 07 · Connecting the dots

"If we solve all this, how does it actually get me ranked?"
Fair question. Here is the chain, layer by layer.

Layer 1 · Knots 1, 2, 3

Sitewide trust: speed passes, one identity, a verifiable licensed expert behind every page. This raises the ranking ceiling for every target at once. Nothing else works while this is broken.

Layer 2 · Knots 4, 5 + internal links

Page-level clarity: each target page tells Google exactly what service, where, by whom, and gives visitors a reason to act. Behavioral signals (clicks, stays, bookings) start feeding rankings back.

Layer 3 · Knot 6

Backlinks: quality links from niche-relevant, well-maintained sites raise the whole site's authority in Google's eyes. Once trust and clarity are in place, this is the push that carries the target pages onto page one.

That is the whole logic: first Google has to trust the site, then each page has to clearly deserve its keyword, then backlinks give it the authority to win. Done in that order, every fix supports the one after it.

"Untying one knot in a tangled rope feels pointless. Untying all six in the right order is how you get an arrow."

Session 08 · Where this goes

You spend your days
untying other people's knots.

Let us untie your website's. Every screenshot is dated. Every number came from your own Search Console, your own pages, and independent tools.

  • You get the "ignore list" from Session 06 free, forever, regardless of what you decide. Nobody should ever scare you with those 2,000 URLs again.
